Name: Legacy System
Description: An existing system with outdated technology stack limited tests and high change risk.

Name: Legacy Hotspot
Description: Module or file with high change frequency and defect density.

Name: Strangler Fig Pattern
Description: Incrementally replace legacy components by routing new behavior through a modern facade.

Name: Anti-Corruption Layer
Description: Isolate a legacy subsystem behind a translation layer to protect new code from legacy constraints.

Name: Technical Debt Cluster
Description: Area with many quick fixes TODOs and workarounds that hinder safe evolution.

Name: Regression Risk
Description: Increased chance of breaking existing behavior when modifying legacy code.

Name: Missing Abstraction
Description: No interface or abstract class separates the legacy module from its callers; violates OCP and DIP forcing clients to couple to concrete implementations.

Name: God Service
Description: A service class accumulates dozens of operations across unrelated concerns; SRP violation common in legacy service layers.

Name: Concrete Dependency
Description: Legacy code directly instantiates collaborators with new instead of receiving them via injection; DIP violation that blocks testability and substitutability.

Name: Change Cascade
Description: Modifying one legacy module forces edits across many unrelated modules; OCP violation proxy measurable by co-change frequency in version history.

Name: Anemic Domain Model
Description: Domain objects carry only data with no behavior; logic leaks into service layers causing SRP and DIP violations throughout the codebase.
